# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment that mounts host directories for our logging agent. The pod needs access to /var/log and /var/lib/docker to collect logs from all containers. This is for our centralized logging system that runs as a DaemonSet.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: logging-agent
  labels:
    app: logging-agent
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: logging-agent
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: logging-agent
    spec:
      tolerations:
      - key: node-role.kubernetes.io/master
        effect: NoSchedule
      containers:
      - name: logging-agent
        image: your-logging-agent-image:latest # Replace with your actual logging agent image
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 200Mi
          requests:
            cpu: 100m
            memory: 200Mi
        volumeMounts:
        - name: varlog
          mountPath: /var/log
        - name: vardockershim
          mountPath: /var/lib/docker
          readOnly: true # Mount /var/lib/docker as read-only as it typically contains socket files and other sensitive data.
        securityContext:
          runAsUser: 0 # Or a specific user if your agent requires it and has permissions
          runAsGroup: 0
          capabilities:
            add: ["SYS_PTRACE"] # Example for some agents, adjust as needed
      volumes:
      - name: varlog
        hostPath:
          path: /var/log
      - name: vardockershim
        hostPath:
          path: /var/lib/docker
      hostNetwork: true # Often needed for logging agents to communicate with other services or the host.
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
